title: One Night Without Sleep Gives You the Immune System of an Obese Person
description: A single 24-hour period without sleep reshapes the immune cells of healthy young adults into inflammatory profiles scientists normally associate with clinical obesity — and it happens before chronic sleep loss has a chance to accumulate. New research identifies the specific monocyte populations responsible, and the mechanism explains why one bad night feels like so much more than tiredness. This is what your immune system is doing while you're awake.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for One Night Without Sleep Gives You the Immune System of an Obese Person
